meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Master SaaS development by solving debugging bottlenecks. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade applications and accelerate your career.
content.answer_block: Debugging paralysis is the primary killer of student projects. By adopting a robust SaaS starter kit, you shift from writing boilerplate to building features. Use Antigravity to scaffold production-ready architecture and Claude Code to analyze stack traces in real-time, effectively automating the resolution of complex logic errors.
content.intro:
You are staring at a terminal overflowing with red text, the cursor blinking in mocking rhythm. The code that worked yesterday is now a labyrinth of dependency conflicts and silent failures. This isn't just a bug; it is the wall every aspiring software engineer hits when the complexity of a real-world SaaS app outgrows their mental model.

But the industry doesn't wait for you to decode stack traces manually. The gap between a student and a senior developer isn't the ability to never write bugs—it's the speed at which they resolve them using hardened architectural patterns. Today, we bridge that gap by replacing fragile, scratch-built foundations with a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, powered by the latest AI-driven debugging workflows.

steps.title: Scaffold with a Hardened Starter Kit
steps.description: Stop reinventing authentication and database schemas. Use a starter kit to establish a production-ready boilerplate that handles the heavy lifting of security and routing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular SaaS boilerplate including Next.js 14, Prisma ORM, and Auth.js. Ensure the architecture follows Domain Driven Design (DDD) to isolate business logic from infrastructure.

steps.title: Automated Dependency Auditing
steps.description: Most bugs stem from mismatched versions. Use Claude Code to audit your package.json against known stable releases to prevent phantom errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project dependencies for version conflicts. Provide a refactored package.json that ensures stability across production and development environments.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'audit dependencies and fix version mismatches'

steps.title: Context-Aware Error Resolution
steps.description: Feed specific error logs into an AI agent that understands your entire codebase context, rather than just the isolated file.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Staff Engineer. Analyze this error trace from my log file and cross-reference it with the controller logic. Identify if the failure is a race condition or a type mismatch.

steps.title: Implementing Defensive Testing
steps.description: Write tests that catch bugs before they reach the browser. Generate unit tests for your core business services automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of Vitest unit tests for my primary service layer. Focus on edge cases where database null returns might occur.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate unit tests for all files in /services'

steps.title: Observability and Telemetry Integration
steps.description: Bugs are easier to solve if you can see them happening. Integrate telemetry to track state changes in your SaaS application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate OpenTelemetry into my existing API routes. Provide a middleware implementation that captures request state and execution time for debugging purposes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'add telemetry middleware to src/app/api'

pro_tips: Always treat your error logs as data: Pipe them into a structured format to make them readable for AI agents.
Never debug in production: Use feature flags to isolate new code paths before full deployment.
Master the 'Why' over the 'How': Ask your AI tools to explain the root cause, not just provide a patch.
Keep your context window clean: Periodically purge unnecessary files from your AI workspace to improve code suggestion accuracy.
Build 'Fail-Fast' mechanisms: Use Zod schemas to validate data at the boundaries, turning logic bugs into clear, catchable runtime errors.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to a staging environment (like Vercel or Railway) and intentionally break a non-critical feature to practice your new AI-assisted debugging workflow.
content.conversion_block: Stop struggling with cryptic errors and start building the future of SaaS. Master these tools, build a portfolio that stands out, and gain the confidence to ship professional-grade software. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
